title: Tri-plex staining for PAX5, CD4, and CSF1R detection in formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) pig tissues
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"70kqf","text":"A protocol for staining of protein (PAX5) and RNA (CD4, CSF1R) in pig tissues.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"cg3bc","text":"Supporting Information","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7qio1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f8qhg","text":"Starting specimens:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ab3sh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dn5ll","text":"Starting samples = FFPE tissues cut to 4 micron thickness and adhered to positively-charged microscopy slides (e.g. SuperFrost Plus Slides; Fisher Scientific 12-550-15). It is crucial that tissues are adequately fixed to prevent tissue degradation but not over-fixed as to over-fragment RNA. Tissues no thicker than 0.5 centimeters should be freshly harvested and placed into 10% neutral-buffered formalin (NBF; 3.7% formaldehyde) or 4% paraformaldehyde (PFA) at a ratio of at least 20 volumes fixative per one volume tissue. Tissues should be fixed for between 16-30 hours at room temperature (RT), followed by immediate transfer to 70% ethanol and processing into FFPE tissue blocks. Fixation times should be optimized for individual tissues and experiments.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bomu7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eb9ve","text":"Assay Controls:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4kg4n","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"afrpu","text":"Here are a few controls you can use to ensure the assay is working correctly: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e8tck","text":"• IHC controls: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"12p3o","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9fklg","text":"     o Negative control (primary antibody only)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6sm00","text":" This slide receives co-detection antibody diluent in place of diluted secondary antibody. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2t55r","text":"     o Negative control (secondary antibody only)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2trkl","text":" This slide receives co-detection antibody diluent in place of diluted primary antibody. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4jsj4","text":"     o Batch control","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5qkro","text":"If performing staining across multiple batches, include serial sections of one tissue in each batch ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eecl1","text":"that has positive staining for PAX5. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5ap5v","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"21evr","text":"• RNA-ISH controls: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"58ard","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4nh09","text":"     o Positive control","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ejfp9","text":"This slide is incubated with PPIB probe.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aemac","text":"     o Negative control","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cg6hd","text":"This slide is incubated with DapB probe.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8md1k","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"s5pe","text":"Assay Variations:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"66o5p","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bmemb","text":"Parameters for some steps (e.g. antigen retrieval, antibody incubations, protease plus incubation, fluorophore/opal incubations, some AMP incubations, counterstaining) may need to be further optimized for different tissues or targets. This protocol is likely applicable to tissues of other species. Consult ACD for optimization assistance. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Bake slides 00:30:00 60 °C . Optional stopping point: store slides in a dry place & use within 1 week.
While slides bake:
Immediately before deparaffinizing:
Submerge slides in fresh xylenes 00:05:00 Room temperature . Optional stopping point: store slides in a dry place & use within 24 hours.
While slides deparaffinize: Turn off dry oven.
Unload slides from vertical slide rack and place on flat surface of bench top.
While slides incubate with Hydrogen Peroxide:
Leave slides in water at Room temperature until decloaker is preheated (< 00:05:00 ).
While slides incubate in 1X target retrieval solution:
Apply hydrophobic barrier around each tissue.
Decant slides and again place flat in slide staining tray.
While slides are incubating with primary antibody:
The next day:
While slides are sitting in PBS-T:
Submerge slide rack in 10% NBF 00:30:00 Room temperature .
While slides are incubating with 10% NBF: Add ~ 200 mL PBS-T to each of three staining dishes.
Transfer slides into EZ-Batch Slide Holder, taking care not to let tissues dry out.
While slides are incubating with protease:
Decant slides (without removing slides from holder).
While slides are incubating with probes:
Decant slides (without removing slides from holder).
Remove slide holder from HybEZ oven/humidifying tray & decant (without removing slides from holder).
Remove slide holder from HybEZ oven/humidifying tray & decant (without removing slides from holder).
Remove slide holder from HybEZ oven/humidifying tray & decant (without removing slides from holder).
Remove slide holder from HybEZ oven/humidifying tray & decant (without removing slides from holder).

Immediately before TSA Vivid Fluorophore 520 incubation: Prepare diluted fluorophore by diluting TSA Vivid Fluorophore 520 into Multiplex TSA Buffer at a dilution of 1:1000 at Room temperature . Total volume to use is dependent on tissue sizes. Make sure to mix reagents before pipetting. Store in the dark due to light sensitivity.
Decant slides (without removing slides from holder).
Remove slide holder from humidifying tray & decant (without removing slides from holder).
Remove slide holder from HybEZ oven/humidifying tray & decant (without removing slides from holder).
Remove slide holder from HybEZ oven/humidifying tray & decant (without removing slides from holder).

Immediately before Opal 650 incubation: Prepare diluted Opal fluorophore by diluting Opal 650 into Multiplex TSA Buffer at a dilution of 1:1000 at RT. Total volume to use is dependent on tissue sizes. Make sure to mix reagents before pipetting. Store in the dark due to light sensitivity.
Decant slides (without removing slides from holder).
Remove slide holder from humidifying tray & decant (without removing slides from holder).
Remove slide holder from HybEZ oven/humidifying tray & decant (without removing slides from holder).
During each incubation:
Decant slides (without removing slides from holder).
Remove slide holder from humidifying tray & decant (without removing slides from holder).
While slides are incubating with protein block:

Immediately before protein detection: Prepare diluted secondary antibody by adding Alexa FluorTM 594 donkey anti-rat IgG to Co-Detection Antibody Diluent at a dilution of 1:500 at Room temperature . Total volume to use is dependent on tissue sizes. Make sure to mix reagents before pipetting. Store in the dark due to light sensitivity.
Decant slides (without removing slides from holder).
While slides are incubating with secondary antibody:
One at a time, remove slides from slide holder and:
Prepare 0.05% PBS-T (can store at Room temperature up to 1 month).

Prepare 1X Co-Detection Target Retrieval solution by adding 1 bottle ( 70 mL ) of Co-Detection Target Retrieval Reagent (10X stock concentration) to 630 mL distilled water (can store at 4 °C up to 1 month).
Add ~ 200 mL xylenes to each of two clearing agent dishes in a fume hood.
Add ~ 200 mL 100% ethanol to each of two staining dishes in a fume hood.
Submerge slides in fresh xylenes 00:05:00 Room temperature .
Submerge slides in fresh 100% ethanol 00:05:00 Room temperature .
Submerge slides in fresh 100% ethanol 00:05:00 Room temperature .
Air dry slides ~ 00:05:00 or until completely dry.

• Prepare decloaking chamber: o Pour 500 mL distilled water into central chamber. o Pour 200 mL distilled water into each left and right staining dishes. o Pour 200 mL prepared 1X Co-Detection Target Retrieval solution into middle staining dish. Turn off dry oven.

Immediately before tissue quenching: Preheat the prepared decloaking chamber, programmed for 00:15:00 at 95 °C . Chamber will take exactly 15 min to preheat, and there will be a 2 min window to add slides before chamber pressurizes & locks.
Incubate with Hydrogen Peroxide 00:10:00 Room temperature . Invert bottle immediately before use; apply drops to completely cover tissues; let incubate on bench top.
Decant slides and transfer to vertical slide rack.
Submerge slide rack in fresh distilled water, dunking 3-5 times.
Submerge slide rack in fresh distilled water, dunking 3-5 times.
Discard deparaffinizing reagents.
Add ~ 200 mL distilled water to each of two staining dishes.
Once decloaker has preheated, submerge slide rack in preheated distilled water 00:00:10 (left dish in decloaker).

Submerge slide rack in preheated 1X Co-Detection Target Retrieval solution 00:15:00 95 °C . o Once slides are placed in center staining dish of decloaker, close the decloaker & wait for alarm to go off in 00:15:00 . Make sure pressure valve is in place to hold pressure when replacing lid.
Release decloaker chamber pressure valve & open chamber.
Submerge slide rack in fresh distilled water, dunking 3-5 times.
Submerge slide rack in fresh distilled water, dunking 3-5 times.
Submerge slide rack in fresh PBS-T, dunking 3-5 times.
Leave slides in PBS-T.
Submerge slide rack in preheated distilled water 00:00:10 (right dish in decloaker).
Discard tissue quenching reagents.
Add ~ 200 mL distilled water to each of two staining dishes.
Add ~ 200 mL PBS-T to one staining dish.
Prepare humidified slide staining tray by adding water to bottom & placing lid on top.

Prepare diluted primary antibody by adding anti-PAX5 antibody to Co-Detection Antibody Diluent at a 1:100 dilution (if stock antibody concentration is 500 μg/ml ) at Room temperature . Total volume to use is dependent on tissue sizes. Make sure to mix reagents before pipetting.
One by one, unload slides from vertical rack submerged in PBS-T.
Dry off only the area around the tissue where a barrier will be drawn with ImmEdge Hydrophobic Barrier Pen. Keep tissue area wet the whole time.
Draw barrier and place slide flat in the slide staining tray.
Using a pipette, apply a small amount of PBS-T within the barrier. Just enough to keep the tissue wet while drawing barriers on remaining slides.

Incubate with diluted primary antibody Overnight at 4 °C . Pipette well to mix immediately before use; pipette appropriate volumes to completely cover tissues and let incubate in slide staining tray with lid closed.
Discard target retrieval and primary antibody reagents.
Remove slides from slide staining tray, decant, and transfer to vertical slide rack.
Submerge slide rack in fresh PBS-T 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Submerge slide rack in fresh PBS-T 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Submerge slide rack in fresh PBS-T 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Add ~ 200 mL PBS-T to each of three staining dishes.
Add ~ 200 mL 10% NBF to one staining dish in a fume hood.
Submerge slide rack in fresh PBS-T 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Submerge slide rack in fresh PBS-T 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Submerge slide rack in fresh PBS-T 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Prepare HybEZ Oven:
Place humidifying paper within the humidity control tray & apply distilled water to fully wet paper.
Place humidifying tray into HybEZ oven and clamp down the gasket to seal.
Preheat oven to 40 °C for at least 00:30:00 before use.

Incubate with Protease Plus 00:15:00 40 °C . Invert bottle immediately before use; apply drops or pipette appropriate volumes to completely cover tissues & transfer slide holder to humidifying tray within HybEZ oven.
Remove slide holder from HybEZ oven/humidifying tray & decant (without removing slides from holder).
Submerge slide holder in fresh distilled water, dunking 3-5 times.
Submerge slide holder in fresh distilled water, dunking 3-5 times.
Empty the slide staining tray used for primary antibody incubation & put away.
Discard antibody cross-linking reagents.
Add ~ 200 mL distilled water to each of two wash trays.
Preheat RNAscope probes to 40 °C for 00:10:00 before use. This can be done by placing them inside the HybEZ oven during protease incubation.
Once preheated, add CD4-C2 probe to CSF1R-C1 probe at a dilution of 1:50. Total volume to use is dependent on tissue sizes. Make sure to mix reagents before pipetting. Place probes back at 4 °C .

Incubate with appropriate RNAscope probe cocktail 02:00:00 40 °C . Invert bottle or pipette well to mix immediately before use; apply drops to completely cover tissues & transfer slide holder to humidifying tray within HybEZ oven.
Remove slide holder from HybEZ oven/humidifying tray & decant (without removing slides from holder).
Submerge slide holder in fresh 1X wash buffer 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Submerge slide holder in fresh 1X wash buffer 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Discard protease reagents.
Prepare 1X wash buffer by adding 1 bottle ( 60 mL ) Wash Buffer (10X stock concentration) to 2.94 L distilled water.
If 10X Wash Buffer solution has a precipitant formed, preheat bottle at 37 °C for 01:00:00 before adding to distilled water.
Will have to prepare another batch of 1X wash buffer later in protocol, after first batch runs out.
Alternatively, prepare both batches at once (120 mL 1X Wash Buffer + 5.88 L distilled water). Store at Room temperature up to one month.

Add ~ 200 mL 1X wash buffer to each of two wash trays. Place AMPs from RNAscope Multiplex Fluorescent Detection kit at Room temperature for at least 00:30:00 before use (should be Room temperature when used).
Incubate with AMP1 00:30:00 40 °C . Invert bottle immediately before use; apply drops to completely cover tissues & transfer slide holder to humidifying tray within HybEZ oven.
Submerge slide holder in fresh 1X wash buffer 00:02:00 Room temperature
Submerge slide holder in fresh 1X wash buffer 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Decant slides (without removing slides from holder).
Incubate with AMP2 00:30:00 40 °C . Invert bottle immediately before use; apply drops to completely cover tissues & transfer slide holder to humidifying tray within HybEZ oven.
Submerge slide holder in fresh 1X wash buffer 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Submerge slide holder in fresh 1X wash buffer 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Decant slides (without removing slides from holder).
Incubate with AMP3 00:15:00 40 °C . Invert bottle immediately before use; apply drops to completely cover tissues & transfer slide holder to humidifying tray within HybEZ oven.
Submerge slide holder in fresh 1X wash buffer 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Submerge slide holder in fresh 1X wash buffer 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Decant slides (without removing slides from holder).
Incubate with HRP-C1 00:15:00 40 °C . Invert bottle immediately before use; apply drops to completely cover tissues & transfer slide holder to humidifying tray within HybEZ oven.
Submerge slide holder in fresh 1X wash buffer 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Submerge slide holder in fresh 1X wash buffer 00:02:00 Room temperature

Incubate with diluted TSA Vivid Fluorophore 520 00:30:00 40 °C . Pipette well to mix immediately before use; pipette appropriate volumes to completely cover tissues & transfer slide holder to humidifying tray within HybEZ oven.
Submerge slide holder in fresh 1X wash buffer 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Submerge slide holder in fresh 1X wash buffer 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Incubate with HRP blocker 00:15:00 40 °C . Invert bottle immediately before use; apply drops to completely cover tissues & transfer slide holder to humidifying tray within HybEZ oven.
Submerge slide holder in fresh 1X wash buffer 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Submerge slide holder in fresh 1X wash buffer 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Decant slides (without removing slides from holder).
Incubate with HRP-C2 00:15:00 40 °C . Invert bottle immediately before use; apply drops to completely cover tissues & transfer slide holder to humidifying tray within HybEZ oven.
Submerge slide holder in fresh 1X wash buffer 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Submerge slide holder in fresh 1X wash buffer 00:02:00 Room temperature

Incubate with diluted Opal 650 00:30:00 40 °C . Pipette well to mix immediately before use; pipette appropriate volumes to completely cover tissues & transfer slide holder to humidifying tray within HybEZ oven.
Submerge slide holder in fresh 1X wash buffer 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Submerge slide holder in fresh 1X wash buffer 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Incubate with HRP blocker 00:15:00 40 °C . Invert bottle immediately before use; apply drops to completely cover tissues & transfer slide holder to humidifying tray within HybEZ oven.
Submerge slide holder in fresh 1X wash buffer 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Submerge slide holder in fresh 1X wash buffer 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Discard reagents from previous incubation step.
Add ~ 200 mL 1X wash buffer to each of two wash trays
Incubate with Protein Block 00:20:00 Room temperature . Invert bottle immediately before use; apply drops to completely cover tissues & transfer slide holder to humidifying tray on bench top.
Submerge slide holder in fresh PBS-T 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Submerge slide holder in fresh PBS-T 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Discard RNA detection reagents.
Add ~ 200 mL PBS-T to each of two wash trays. Turn off HybEZ oven.

Incubate with diluted secondary antibody 01:00:00 Room temperature . Pipette well to mix immediately before use; pipette appropriate volumes to completely cover tissues & transfer slide holder to humidifying tray on bench top.
Remove slide holder from humidifying tray & decant (without removing slides from holder).
Submerge slide holder in fresh PBS-T 00:02:00 Room temperature .
Submerge slide holder in fresh PBS-T 00:02:00 Room temperature
Discard protein blocking reagents.
Add ~ 200 mL PBS-T to each of two wash trays
Apply DAPI 00:00:30 Room temperature .
Decant slide to remove DAPI.
Mount slides by adding 2-4 drops of ProLong Gold antifade mounting media to each slide, followed by application of a #1.5 cover glass. Remove bubbles from tissue by applying pressure to cover glass.
Place slides flat in a dry, dark space to air dry 00:30:00 Room temperature .
Store at 4 °C and image within two weeks.
creator: Crystal L Loving
